Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: You are given a process model represented in the DECLARE constraint language The model is specified as a Python dictionary with particular keys corresponding to different DECLARE constraints Some constraints involve only one activity while others involve pairs of activities

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to generate a new declare_model based on an example model given The example is small existence of StartApplication and FinalDecision init StartApplication coexistence StartApplication to FinalDecision response StartApplication to RequestAdditionalInfo succession RequestAdditionalInfo to FinalDecision But we need to add constraints to mitigate bias for a loan application process with sensitive attributes

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We are free to add activities and constraints The instruction says Insert new DECLARE constraints into the model that enforce fairness We should consider adding activities that represent checks or decisions with sensitive attributes Perhaps the process already has some activities like CheckApplicantRace CheckApplicantAge CheckApplicantGender Approve Reject RequestAdditionalInfo ManualReview BiasMitigationCheck and so on

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We should be careful we are extending an existing model so we cannot remove existing constraints we only add new ones

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The initial model likely is just an example we need to output an updated model with new constraints Our goal is to add fairness constraints to limit bias

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The instruction suggests adding constraints like Coexistence if a decision step occurs for a sensitive applicant ensure a ManualReview activity always coexists in the trace Response or Succession constraints ensure that certain decision steps cannot immediately follow an activity disclosing sensitive attributes without first performing a BiasMitigationCheck Non succession constraints prevent a direct succession from a sensitive attribute event to a decision event

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Thus we need to define activities such as CheckApplicantRace CheckApplicantAge CheckApplicantGender maybe as activities that reveal sensitive info ManualReview BiasMitigationCheck Approve Reject

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The given model already includes StartApplication FinalDecision RequestAdditionalInfo We could map FinalDecision to Approve or Reject Possibly we can treat FinalDecision as a generic decision and add specific decision types But the existing model uses FinalDecision so we should avoid conflict

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: It is simpler to use the existing FinalDecision and treat it as the decision event rather than introducing Approve and Reject which might conflict with the existing constraints

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We will add activities CheckApplicantRace CheckApplicantAge CheckApplicantGender and ManualReview

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We will add response constraints CheckApplicantRace to ManualReview CheckApplicantAge to ManualReview CheckApplicantGender to ManualReview

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We will add a precedence constraint ManualReview to FinalDecision so that if FinalDecision occurs then ManualReview must have occurred before

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We will add nonchainsuccession constraints from each sensitive check to FinalDecision to prevent the final decision from immediately following a sensitive attribute check

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We may also add existence constraints for CheckApplicantRace CheckApplicantAge CheckApplicantGender to make these checks mandatory and ensure the fairness control flow is always activated

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We need to ensure that the new constraints do not conflict with existing ones The model remains satisfiable with an ordering such as StartApplication then the sensitive checks and RequestAdditionalInfo then ManualReview then FinalDecision

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The updated dictionary should keep all original top level keys and preserve the nested DECLARE structure with support 1 point 0 and confidence 1 point 0 for every added constraint

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The final updated model therefore adds the new activities under existence adds the three response links to ManualReview adds the precedence link from ManualReview to FinalDecision and adds the three nonchainsuccession links from the sensitive checks to FinalDecision while leaving the original constraints unchanged
